item.title: Passenger on flight from Newark tried to open door mid-flight, pilot says
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Newark Liberty Airport was forced to make an emergency landing after a passenger allegedly tried to open a door.

item.title: Rubio says Iran imposing tolls on Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable"
item.description: The Secretary of State tempered optimism for a deal to end the Iran war, calling Iran's bid to "create a tolling system" in the Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable."

item.title: Ebola treatment center in Congo set on fire as fear and anger grow
item.description: People set fire to an Ebola treatment center in a town at the heart of the outbreak in eastern Congo as fear and anger grow over a health crisis that doctors are struggling to contain.

item.title: 2 climbers die on Everest as record-holder warns of overcrowding
item.description: At least five climbers have died during this Everest season. A U.S. and a Czech climber died on Mount Makalu earlier this month.

item.title: Stephen Colbert says farewell to "The Late Show" in heartfelt finale
item.description: Stephen Colbert hosted "The Late Show" for the final time Thursday night as the franchise came to an end after 33 years.

item.title: Body found stuffed in barrel linked to Belgrade police chief
item.description: Police found the body of a man stuffed in a barrel following a shooting in a restaurant that led to the arrest of Belgrade's police chief, prosecutors said.

item.title: Feeding Our Future fraud ringleader Aimee Bock sentenced to 41+ years
item.description: Aimee Bock, the convicted ringleader of the $250 million Feeding Our Future fraud scheme in Minnesota, was sentenced to more than 40 years in prison on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. doctor with Ebola feared he "wasn't going to make it" before evacuation
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford was working with the missionary group Serge in Congo when he was infected with Ebola.

item.title: Primary fight in key California district highlights questions about Democrats' future
item.description: The latest fight over the Democratic Party's direction is playing out in a competitive California House primary, as progressives accuse party leaders of trying to muscle a moderate past a Latino challenger in a heavily Hispanic district.

item.title: Inside the Congressional Record: How history gets made as D.C. sleeps
item.description: The production of the Congressional Record is one of the unseen cogs in the congressional machine, arriving with little fanfare like a newspaper on the Capitol's doorstep every day.

item.title: New site aims to track $12.5B in air traffic control system upgrades
item.description: The Department of Transportation is rolling out a new website to track progress in the sprawling effort to modernize the nation's air traffic control system​.

item.title: CBS News Radio signing off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio is signing off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.

item.title: Iran mulls latest U.S. proposal as Trump says war is in "final stages"
item.description: Iran says it's considering the latest U.S. peace offer, as President Trump says he's willing to wait "a couple of days" for a response.

item.title: House GOP pulls Iran war resolution that appeared to have enough support to pass
item.description: Republicans are struggling to find the votes to dismiss legislation that would compel President Trump to withdraw from the war with Iran.

item.title: DNC releases 2024 election autopsy on how Democrats lost
item.description: The Democratic National Committee released a long-awaited autopsy on the 2024 election that party chair Ken Martin has kept under wraps for months.

item.title: Navy's acting secretary says Taiwan arms sale put on "pause" over Iran war
item.description: The acting secretary of the U.S. Navy said Thursday that arm sales to Taiwan had been put on "pause" to ensure that the American military had sufficient munitions for its Iran operations.

item.title: GOP senators press Blanche on "anti-weaponization fund" in tense meeting
item.description: Some Republican senators openly expressed their concerns about the Justice Department's new "anti-weaponization fund" in a tense meeting Thursday with Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche.

item.title: Teen attackers in mosque shooting were wallowing in nihilistic hate
item.description: "These subjects did not discriminate in who they hated," said Mark Remily, special agent in charge of the FBI's San Diego Field Office.

item.title: Several people watched San Diego attack live on video calls, recordings show
item.description: At least three people watched a livestream as gunmen filmed their deadly attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday. One viewer urged another to alert law enforcement, but it is not clear if anyone did.

item.title: Single Gen Z women outpacing men in home buying
item.description: Data from the National Association of Realtors shows that 35% of single women ages 18 to 26 are buying homes compared to just 18% of single Gen Z men. However, as a whole, the age group is struggling to keep up with others when it comes to owning a home. Jessica Lautz, the deputy chief economist at the National Association of Realtors, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: What's holding the U.S. and Iran apart on a peace deal?
item.description: The Iranian government is reviewing the latest U.S. proposal to end the war. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean analyzes the situation.

item.title: A theater summer camp for adults
item.description: Summer Camp isn't just for kids anymore. Each year, more adults are heading back to camp for a chance to sing, dance and take a break from burnout. Gwen Baumgardner unpacks the growing trend for grown-ups.

item.title: Breaking down the Democrats' 2024 election autopsy report
item.description: The Democratic National Committee has released the long-awaited autopsy report on its 2024 election loss. CBS News political contributor Joel Payne breaks down the results.

item.title: Trump onboards more than 80 new immigration judges to speed up deportation cases
item.description: CBS News has learned that the Trump administration brought on more than 80 new federal immigration judges this week to speed up deportation cases. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the exclusive report.

item.title: CBS News Radio prepares for final sign off after nearly 100 years of broadcasting
item.description: CBS News Radio will sign off this Friday after nearly a century on the air. Bradley Blackburn sat down with familiar voices to look back and listen.

item.title: Is Trump's $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization fund" legal? Experts weigh in.
item.description: The new fund to provide payouts to those who say the legal system was "weaponized" against them raised immediate questions about its legality, implementation and enforcement.

item.title: Sister of Cuban military conglomerate chief arrested in Florida, Rubio says
item.description: The sister of the head of a company that is accused of having close ties to Cuban government operations has been arrested in Miami, federal authorities announced.

item.title: 5/21: CBS Evening News
item.description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies at 41; an Ebola scare causes travel chaos.

item.title: Congress delays votes amid GOP opposition to new DOJ fund
item.description: The House and Senate will both leave Washington for their Memorial Day recess without voting on a reconciliation package to fund federal immigration agencies.

item.title: DOJ onboards largest-ever class of immigration judges in deportation push
item.description: The additions come after the ouster of dozens of immigration judges across the country by the Trump administration over the past year.
